meta.title: Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: End of day reflection tool. Deterministic. No API calls at runtime.

nodes.text: Hey. Before you close the laptop — just a few questions about today.
Pick one word for today.

options.label: A
options.text: Good

options.label: B
options.text: Okay

options.label: C
options.text: Hard

options.label: D
options.text: Draining

nodes.rule: if Q1.signal is victor go to Q2a, else go to Q2b

nodes.text: Something went well today. What actually made that happen?

options.label: A
options.text: I put in the prep beforehand

options.label: B
options.text: I kept going when it got uncomfortable

options.label: C
options.text: I changed approach when the first one failed

options.label: D
options.text: Honestly the team carried it

nodes.text: Was there a point today where you nearly stopped but kept going anyway?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes and it paid off

options.label: B
options.text: Yes but I am still not sure it was right

options.label: C
options.text: Not really, today was manageable

options.label: D
options.text: I did stop on something and it was fine

nodes.text: When things went sideways today, where did your head go first?

options.label: A
options.text: I went straight to what I could have done differently

options.label: B
options.text: I blamed someone else, at least at first

options.label: C
options.text: I sat and waited for it to get fixed

options.label: D
options.text: I felt like the day was working against me

nodes.text: Was there even one moment today where the choice was fully yours?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes and I made a decent call

options.label: B
options.text: Yes and I made a bad one, I know that now

options.label: C
options.text: I genuinely felt like I had no real options

options.label: D
options.text: I did not look at it that way

nodes.text: You called today '{Q1.answer}'. Somewhere in that day there was a moment where how you responded was up to you. Even a small one counts.
OK. Now less about what happened and more about what you brought to it.
Which of these sounds most like your day?

options.label: A
options.text: I helped someone out with something that was not my job

options.label: B
options.text: I did more than what was asked without making a thing of it

options.label: C
options.text: I felt like my effort went unnoticed by the people who matter

options.label: D
options.text: I was annoyed that some people were not pulling their weight

nodes.rule: if Q3.signal is contribution go to Q4a, else go to Q4b

nodes.text: What did you actually give today that no one asked for?

options.label: A
options.text: Time to help someone who was stuck

options.label: B
options.text: A suggestion that could make something work better

options.label: C
options.text: A bit of energy when the room needed it

options.label: D
options.text: Extra time to finish something properly

nodes.text: Did anyone say thank you — and did it matter to you whether they did?

options.label: A
options.text: They did and it honestly made no difference to me

options.label: B
options.text: They did and I will not pretend that was not nice

options.label: C
options.text: They did not and I was fine with that

options.label: D
options.text: They did not and I noticed that I cared

nodes.text: What felt most off today?

options.label: A
options.text: The people who should have noticed my work did not

options.label: B
options.text: Someone else got credit for something I had a hand in

options.label: C
options.text: I ended up carrying more than my share

options.label: D
options.text: I expected some feedback and it never came

nodes.text: Was there anything you did today that you did not need anyone to see?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes actually, and thinking about it now changes the picture a bit

options.label: B
options.text: Yes but that does not cancel out what felt unfair

options.label: C
options.text: Not really, most of what I did was just expected of me

options.label: D
options.text: I need to sit with that question

nodes.text: Quick check: did you keep score today based on what you gave, or on what came back to you? Worth knowing which one it was.
Last section. Zoom out a bit.
Think about the toughest part of today. Who is in that picture?

options.label: A
options.text: Mostly me, it was my problem

options.label: B
options.text: The team, we were all in it

options.label: C
options.text: One particular person who had it rougher

options.label: D
options.text: The customer or end user on the other side

nodes.rule: if Q5.signal is altrocentric go to Q6a, else go to Q6b

nodes.text: How did what you did today land with the people around you?

options.label: A
options.text: I made someone's work or day a bit easier

options.label: B
options.text: I checked in on someone who looked like they needed it

options.label: C
options.text: I probably added to someone's stress without meaning to

options.label: D
options.text: I did not really clock it either way

nodes.text: When you helped someone today, what was actually driving that?

options.label: A
options.text: It just seemed like the right thing to do

options.label: B
options.text: I could see they needed it and I had the space

options.label: C
options.text: Part of me hoped they would return the favour

options.label: D
options.text: I am not sure, it was more automatic than anything

nodes.text: Was there anyone around you today who was having a harder time than you?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes and I made sure they knew I noticed

options.label: B
options.text: Yes but I was too wrapped up in my own stuff

options.label: C
options.text: I did not pick up on it if there was

options.label: D
options.text: Everyone seemed alright from where I was sitting

nodes.text: Is there someone from today whose experience of it you actually do not know?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes and I should probably have asked

options.label: B
options.text: Probably, but today was not the day for that

options.label: C
options.text: No, I had a decent read on the room

options.label: D
options.text: That is a new way to think about it for me

nodes.text: The days people remember are not usually the ones where everything went well for them personally. They remember the ones where they were actually present for someone.
That is three questions done. You know yourself a bit better than you did ten minutes ago.

nodes.rule: combine axis1_signal + axis2_signal + axis3_signal to pick closing insight

nodes.text: Today: [{axis1_signal}] · [{axis2_signal}] · [{axis3_signal}]

closing_insights.victor+contribution+altrocentric: You showed up well today — for yourself and for other people. Do that again.
closing_insights.victor+contribution+self: You did good work and gave more than required. Tomorrow try looking up and seeing who else is in the room.
closing_insights.victor+entitlement+altrocentric: You noticed the people around you which is good. The next step is thinking about what you can put in before thinking about what you are owed.
closing_insights.victor+entitlement+self: You handled your day well. Worth asking tomorrow: what can I give here, not just what do I deserve.
closing_insights.victim+contribution+altrocentric: Rough day, but you still showed up for others. That is not nothing.
closing_insights.victim+contribution+self: You gave something today even when the day was hard. Tomorrow, look up once and see if someone else needs it.
closing_insights.victim+entitlement+altrocentric: You were aware of others which counts. When the days feel heavier, one small thing you do for someone else tends to shift the whole frame.
closing_insights.victim+entitlement+self: Today was heavy. Fine. Tomorrow pick one thing: respond instead of react, give one thing, notice one person. Just one.
